title: sagina maritima var. filiformis
intro: Sagina maritima var. filiformis, also known as sea pearlwort, is a low-growing, succulent perennial plant that thrives in coastal areas and is characterized by its delicate, white or pink flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of sea pearlwort are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and provide protection against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
